takeaways: Senior housing momentum is unmistakable — occupancy hit 89.5% in Q1 (19th straight quarter of gains), 2025 transaction volume reached $24B, and REITs like Welltower and Ventas now source 80%+ of deals off-market.

Powell's term expired May 15 — the Fed is expected to pause early before a new chair potentially resumes cuts toward a 3.00–3.25% range. GSEs raised 2026 multifamily lending caps 20% to $88B each, reopening capital flows.

Proptech is booming on AI — January 2026 alone saw $1.7B invested (+176% YoY), with agentic AI moving from experimentation to operational deployment in procurement, pricing, and asset management across CRE.

items.headline: REITs Dominate Off-Market Senior Living M&A in Q1 2026
items.summary: Welltower sourced 37 of its 41 Q1 transactions off-market, while roughly two-thirds of Ventas' $1.7B senior living investments came off-market. The data shows public REITs leveraging speed-to-deal and operator relationships to lock up portfolios before they hit broader markets, raising the bar for private bidders like Lloyd Jones.

items.headline: LCS-Vi Merger Creates 130-Community Senior Living Operator
items.summary: LCS and Vi closed their previously announced merger, creating a combined company managing 130 communities across 29 states with above-90% average occupancy in their life plan communities. The deal consolidates two of the largest CCRC operators and signals continued operator-side scale plays in luxury senior living.

items.headline: Health Care REIT Drops $425M on 3 Luxury Senior Communities
items.summary: A health care REIT made its senior living debut with a $425M acquisition of three luxury communities, underscoring the high-end thesis that capital is chasing as boomer demand strengthens. The luxury segment continues to see compressed cap rates and intense REIT competition for Class-A assets.

items.headline: Senior Housing Occupancy Hits 89.5% — 19th Straight Quarter of Gains
items.summary: Q1 2026 senior housing occupancy reached 89.5%, with stabilized occupancy surpassing 90% for the first time since late 2017. Active adult occupancy averaged 91.2% across 125 markets, and occupied units climbed by 3,000 to 637,000 — the industry has now added more units than it has lost for 19 consecutive quarters.

items.headline: Janus Living Lines Up $400M in Senior Living Buys After IPO
items.summary: Newly public Janus Living has earmarked $400M for senior living acquisitions, joining NHI (which acquired a 7-community Colorado portfolio for $106.9M in May) and Citrine (which crossed $100M in senior living investments) in a busy dealbook quarter. Private capital remains active alongside REIT activity.

items.headline: Powell's Fed Term Expires May 15 — Pause Expected Before Chair Transition
items.summary: Jerome Powell's term as Fed chair ended yesterday (May 15). Consensus is the Fed pauses early before a new chair potentially resumes cuts to bring overnight rates toward 3.00–3.25%. The Fed cut 25 bps to a 3.50–3.75% range at its final 2025 meeting, totaling 175 bps of easing since September 2024 — a meaningful tailwind for CRE refinancings.

items.headline: Fannie and Freddie Raise 2026 Multifamily Lending Caps 20% to $88B Each
items.summary: Fannie Mae and Freddie Mac will each allow $88B in multifamily loans in 2026, a 20% increase that signals an open agency debt market for senior housing and apartments. Banks are also re-entering the debt market as the yield curve steepens, with all debt sources expected to remain active through 2026.

items.headline: CRE Transaction Volume Set to Climb 15–20% in 2026
items.summary: Colliers forecasts a 15–20% increase in CRE sales volume in 2026 as institutional and cross-border capital re-enters the market. The 10-Year Treasury has stabilized around 4.00–4.25% since mid-2025, with cap rates for most property types expected to compress 5–15 bps and good-quality assets seeing greater compression.

items.headline: Multifamily Investment Hits $170B Over 12 Months as Vacancy Peaks
items.summary: Apartment investment volume over the 12 months ending March 2026 totaled $170.4B, outpacing 2024 by ~10%. Vacancy ticked up to 6.8% (from 6.5%) but Q1 deliveries collapsed to 31,055 units versus a three-year quarterly average of 80,400, suggesting vacancy has reached its cycle peak. Average apartment cap rate sits at 5.8% — tightest among major property types.

items.headline: PropTech VC Investment Hits $1.7B in January 2026, Up 176% YoY
items.summary: Venture capital firms poured roughly $1.7B into proptech in January 2026 alone — a 176% jump versus January 2025 — after the sector closed 2025 with $16.7B raised (up 67.9% YoY). AI-centered proptech is growing at a 42% annualized rate, nearly double the 24% rate of non-AI peers, with four new unicorns minted recently.

items.headline: Agentic AI Goes Operational in Retail and CRE Workflows
items.summary: Agentic AI is moving from pilot to production in 2026, with retailers and real estate operators deploying AI agents for procurement, pricing, inventory and event programming. ICSC reports operators are reshaping cost structure and competitive strategy as autonomous agents replace point tools — a meaningful operational lever for senior housing operators in 2026.

items.headline: Real Estate Fraud Hits $275M as PropTech Pivots to Defense
items.summary: The FBI logged over 12,000 real estate fraud complaints in 2025 totaling $275M+ in reported losses, much of it AI-enabled. Proptech vendors are rapidly pivoting toward AI-driven fraud detection in rental and mortgage workflows — a category to watch for senior housing operators handling deposits and applications.

items.headline: Atlanta Fed: AI Drove 1.8% Labor Productivity Gain in 2025
items.summary: A new Atlanta Fed working paper of 6,000+ executives finds AI delivered 1.8% mean labor productivity growth in 2025, with stronger gains expected in 2026 — concentrated in high-skill services and finance. Technical roles are forecast to grow 0.62% in 2026 and 1.35% by 2028, while aggregate employment is expected to decline under 0.4% due to AI.

items.headline: Leader Burnout Surges: 71% Stressed, 40% Considering Quitting
items.summary: DDI reports 71% of leaders are reporting increased stress from their roles, and 40% of those stressed leaders are thinking of quitting — coinciding with US layoffs hitting a 10-year high. Leadership development remains HR's top priority heading into 2026, with enabling innovation now a parallel mandate.

items.headline: Half of Talent Leaders Plan to Add Autonomous AI Agents in 2026
items.summary: Korn Ferry data shows more than half of talent leaders plan to add autonomous AI agents to their teams in 2026. The shift accelerates an already-flattening org structure trend and puts a premium on human-centered leadership — managing teams that mix human and agent workers becomes a core capability.

items.headline: Deloitte: 79% of Tech Leaders Now Own Enterprise Value Delivery
items.summary: Deloitte's 2026 Global Technology Leadership Study finds 79% of tech leaders report enterprise value delivery is their top priority — yet 42% report low or no ROI on AI investments to date. The gap between mandate and measurable return is the headline tension for IT leaders this year.

items.headline: McKinsey: Three Tectonic Forces Reshaping Organizations in 2026
items.summary: McKinsey's State of Organizations 2026 identifies three forces redefining how companies operate: AI integration, talent scarcity in technical roles, and the need for adaptive leadership amid record layoffs. Flatter structures and continuous learning are emerging as the dominant operating model.

items.headline: 89% of Employees Link Wellbeing to Higher Performance
items.summary: Wellhub's 2026 State of Work-Life-Wellness study finds 89% of employees say prioritizing wellbeing makes them perform better at work. Wellness is shifting from perk to performance lever, with employees moving away from the work-hard-play-hard mentality and building lives around wellbeing.

items.headline: 'Third Places' Become Core to Worker Wellness — 91% Say They Help
items.summary: Wellness-focused 'third places' — gyms, yoga studios, and wellness hubs outside home and office — are emerging as central to professional wellbeing. 91% of employees say these spaces help them handle work pressures more effectively, a relevant trend for active adult and senior living amenity design.

items.headline: 'Emotional Fitness' Emerges as the 2026 Wellness Category
items.summary: Global Wellness Summit names 'emotional fitness' a new 2026 health category, pairing cognitive performance with emotional resilience. Personalization is the through-line: biomarker-based nutrition, AI wellness coaches, and longevity/metabolic health are moving from biohacking niche to mainstream lifestyle spend.
